crime.value: Poisoning murder - John Demeo was killed by poisoned tea
crime.quote: Mrs. Demeo finally confessed to poisoning John Demeo

victim.value: John Demeo, a senior student who had recently asked Joanie out and was competing for Melissa's attention
victim.quote: He brought me in there so he could ask me out on a date. I said no.

closed_world.value: The tea party/gathering at Mrs. Demeo's house where the poisoned tea was served
closed_world.quote: It was okay tea, but it definitely wasn't as good as Mrs. Demeo's

culprit_and_motive.value: Mrs. Demeo (stepmother) poisoned John, possibly related to inheritance since she wasn't his 'favorite thing'
culprit_and_motive.quote: I wouldn't be surprised if she'd killed the old man too, only to find out that she wasn't his favorite thing

resolution.value: Police interrogation leads to confession, though Mrs. Demeo later flees the country after posting bail
resolution.quote: During interrogation, Mrs. Demeo finally confessed to poisoning John Demeo

investigator.value: Jacky, a junior student who analyzes the situation and presents the theory to Detective Stern
investigator.quote: Detective Stern put an end to Jacky's theorizing

suspect_architecture.value: Three suspects with distinct motives: Mrs. Demeo (stepmother with inheritance/hatred motive), Joanie (romantic rejection), and John himself (suicide possibility). Each has clear differentiation in relationship to victim.
suspect_architecture.quote: Mrs. Demeo finally confessed to poisoning John Demeo

red_herring.value: The romantic triangle between John, Joanie, and potential dating conflicts serves as misdirection from the family inheritance motive that drives the actual murder.
red_herring.quote: He brought me in there so he could ask me out on a date. I said no.

clue_fairness.value: The tea taste difference is presented to the reader through Jacky's direct observation - he notices Mrs. Demeo's tea lacks the almond aftertaste, foreshadowing the poisoning method.
clue_fairness.quote: It didn't have that almond aftertaste.

social_world.value: Military academy hierarchy with seniors/juniors, family inheritance structures, and institutional authority (Detective Stern, military funeral protocols) shape the power dynamics.
social_world.quote: I'm a senior. Do you really think Melissa would go out with a Junior?

reveal_mechanic.value: The poisoned tea is revealed through taste comparison - Jacky notices the missing almond aftertaste in the tea, providing the technical evidence that demonstrates the murder method.
reveal_mechanic.quote: It definitely wasn't as good as Mrs. Demeo's. It didn't have that almond aftertaste.

media_and_audience.value: Military funeral ceremony with full honors, orphanage institutional response, and legal system processing (bail, lawyer defenses) show how institutions package and respond to the crime.
media_and_audience.quote: the Army afforded him a full military burial. It was complete with the twenty-one gun salute

investigator_wound.value: Jacky's romantic feelings for Melissa create emotional vulnerability as he investigates a case involving romantic triangles and rejection, mirroring his own unrequited situation.
